skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI with apps for tweets, likes, retweets, DMs, and follows
skill.summary: Post tweets, like, retweet, send DMs, and follow users on Twitter/X using the inference.sh CLI platform
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 37 external command patterns and 6 network URLs, but all are false positives. The findings are documentation examples in SKILL.md showing CLI usage, not executable code. The skill uses allowed-tools restriction (Bash(infsh *)) limiting execution to the inference.sh CLI only. Weak crypto findings at lines 3 and 27 are false positives matching description text. Low risk due to external service dependency on inference.sh platform.

low_findings.title: External CLI Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill relies on inference.sh external CLI service for all Twitter/X operations. Users must authenticate with inference.sh platform and trust their data handling practices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear dependency on external service - all operations route through inference.sh CLI which handles API authentication and requests

content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X presence requires repetitive posting and engagement tasks. This skill automates tweets, likes, retweets, DMs, and follows through the inference.sh CLI platform.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets to Twitter/X via CLI command
Create posts with media attachments (images, videos)
Like and retweet content by tweet ID
Follow users and retrieve profile information
Delete tweets and retrieve tweet details
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account authentication
Cannot perform advanced Twitter Analytics or advertising operations
Media posts require externally hosted URLs, not local file uploads
Rate limits and API restrictions depend on inference.sh platform tier

use_cases.title: Social Media Manager
use_cases.description: Schedule and post content across multiple Twitter accounts, automate engagement with followers, and manage community interactions efficiently
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers handling multiple client accounts

use_cases.title: Content Creator Workflow
use_cases.description: Automatically post AI-generated images and videos to Twitter with captions, creating streamlined content publishing pipelines
use_cases.target_user: Digital creators and AI artists promoting their work

use_cases.title: Developer Announcement Automation
use_cases.description: Integrate Twitter posting into CI/CD pipelines to announce releases, deployment updates, and project milestones automatically

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a tweet with the text: "Just shipped a new feature! Check it out at [link]"

prompt_templates.title: Share AI-Generated Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Twitter post with an image. Use the text "AI-generated sunset landscape" and attach the image from the previous generation step
prompt_templates.scenario: Posting AI artwork or generated media with caption

prompt_templates.title: Engagement Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest tweet from [username] and like it, then retweet it with a comment saying "Great insights on this topic"
prompt_templates.scenario: Automated engagement with specific accounts or content

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Content Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [subject], then post it to Twitter with an engaging caption that includes relevant hashtags for [topic] audience
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end content creation and publishing workflow

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying "Hello from automation!"
output_examples.output: Tweet posted successfully. Tweet ID: 1234567890123456789. Posted at 2026-03-14T10:30:00Z
Tweet liked successfully. Author: @username. Like count: 157
User: OpenAI. Followers: 2.5M. Bio: Creating safe AGI that benefits all of humanity. Verified account.

best_practices: Always review generated content before posting to ensure accuracy and brand alignment
Implement rate limiting in workflows to avoid hitting Twitter API limits or appearing as spam
Store tweet IDs in a tracking system for later reference, analytics, or deletion if needed
anti_patterns: Do not automate mass-following or aggressive engagement that violates Twitter terms of service
Avoid posting identical content repeatedly which may trigger spam detection
Do not share sensitive information, credentials, or private data in automated tweets

faq.question: What do I need to start using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI installed and an authenticated account. Run infsh login to authenticate. Add the skill with: npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools

faq.question: Can I schedule tweets for future posting?
faq.answer: The skill posts immediately. For scheduling, integrate with external scheduler tools or cron jobs to trigger the CLI commands at specific times

faq.question: How do I post images or videos?
faq.answer: Use the x/post-create app with a media_url parameter pointing to a publicly accessible URL. Generate media first using AI image or video generation skills

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but inference.sh may have usage limits or pricing tiers for API calls. Check inference.sh pricing for current rates

faq.question: Can I manage multiple Twitter accounts?
faq.answer: Authentication is per inference.sh account. Managing multiple Twitter accounts requires separate inference.sh authentications or checking if inference.sh supports account switching

faq.question: What happens if a tweet fails to post?
faq.answer: The CLI returns an error with details. Common causes include rate limits, content policy violations, or authentication issues. Check the error message for specific resolution steps
